The 1984 massacres are a clear case of violence organised against a community by a political party for electoral gain. But the facts have never been subjected to an honest, independent inquiry. The Misra and the Nanavati commissions sidestepped the question of a larger conspiracy. If the conspiracy had been properly investigated, it is likely that testimony such as Avtar Gill’s statement about Arun Nehru would have come to light much earlier.
The failure of these inquiries, after thirty years and nine commissions, indicates that the Indian state is not institutionally equipped to ensure justice for victims of communal violence. If justice remains impossible for the victims of 1984, when the violence took place in the national capital and where the evidence is so ample, it seems likely that justice will continue to elude the vast majority of such victims anywhere in the country.
Correction:
The print version of this article incorrectly stated that Central Police Reserve Force personnel arrived to restore order in Trilokpuri, Delhi, on 3 October 1984. They arrived on 3 November.
The Caravan
regrets the error.

title: How current gold interest rates affect the amount you can borrow today
content_html:
Gold loans remain one of the quickest ways to access funds without selling your gold jewellery. With Bajaj Finance, you can apply for an online gold loan with minimal documentation and receive funds quickly. However, before borrowing, it is important to understand how current interest rates can influence your loan experience and overall borrowing cost.

While the value and purity of your gold determine the loan amount you may qualify for, the interest rate affects how affordable that loan will be throughout the repayment period. Understanding these dynamics can help you make better borrowing decisions and manage your finances more effectively

What the gold interest rate means for your loan amount

When you borrow against your gold jewellery, ornaments or coins, the lender charges interest on the loan amount. This interest becomes a key part of the total amount you repay over the loan tenure.

A lower interest rate generally reduces the overall cost of borrowing, allowing you to manage repayments more comfortably. On the other hand, a higher rate increases the amount you pay overtime. Even if the loan amount remains the same, changes in interest rates can significantly affect your repayment obligations.

This is why understanding the loan terms and the applicable rate before applying for an online gold loan is important.

How the current gold interest rate affects your borrowing decision

The current gold interest rate directly impacts the affordability of your loan. When rates are competitive, you can borrow with greater confidence, knowing that your repayment burden may remain manageable. Higher rates, however, can increase the overall cost of borrowing and affect your monthly budget.

Interest rates also influence the repayment structure you choose. For example, borrowers may prefer shorter tenures or faster repayment schedules when rates are higher to reduce the total interest paid. During periods of favourable rates, you may have greater flexibility in selecting a repayment option that suits your financial needs.

Understanding the prevailing gold loan market conditions can therefore help you decide when and how much to borrow.

Loan-to-value ratio and RBI limits

The loan-to-value ratio, or LTV, is the percentage of the assessed gold value that can be offered as a loan. The maximum LTV is capped at 85%, subject to regulatory and lender-level conditions. For consumption loans, the maximum LTV eligible per borrower depends on the loan amount

Current LTV limits for consumption gold loans:

- For loans up to Rs. 2.5 lakh, the LTV limit is 75%

- For loans above Rs. 2.5 lakh and up to Rs. 5 lakh, the LTV limit is 80%

- For loans above Rs. 5 lakh and up to Rs. 2 crore, the LTV limit is 85%

Let us say your assessed gold value is Rs. 4 lakh. If your loan falls in the first slab, the maximum eligible amount may be Rs. 3 lakh at 75% LTV. If the applicable slab permits 80%, the amount can go up to Rs. 3.2 lakh, subject to lender checks.

The gold interest rate does not change the RBI LTV cap. However, it can influence the amount you choose to borrow and, in some cases, the lender’s assessment of repayment suitability.

Role of eligibility in final sanction

The value of gold is important, but it is not the only deciding factor. Lenders follow defined gold loan eligibility criteria before approving the loan. These criteria may include your age, KYC documents, ownership of gold, purity of pledged gold, and the lender’s internal risk policy.

The eligibility criteria for a gold loan with Bajaj Finance are simple and easy to meet. To apply, you must be between 21 and 80 years of age and own gold jewellery or ornaments with a purity ranging from 18 to 22 karats. You can also pledge gold coins of up to 24 karat purity, subject to the lender's applicable terms and conditions.

Conclusion

The gold interest rate affects the amount you can borrow today by shaping the repayment burden, even though the pledged gold value and LTV limits set the main borrowing ceiling. Your final loan amount depends on purity, net gold weight, approved reference valuation, applicable RBI LTV limits, lender policy, and gold loan eligibility criteria. Since lenders such as Bajaj Finance use the lower of the previous day’s closing price or the 30-day average closing price from approved sources, the current market price alone does not decide your loan amount.

title: Eligibility For A Personal Loan Explained With Income, Age And Credit Factors
content_html:
Understanding the eligibility for a personal loan can feel a bit like planning a trip to a new country. There are rules to follow, documents to carry, and a few checkpoints along the way. The good news is that once you know what lenders look for, the process becomes much simpler and far less stressful. This guide breaks down personal loan eligibility using everyday language, focusing on income, age and credit-related factors so you can plan ahead with confidence.

What does eligibility for a personal loan really mean

The eligibility for a personal loan refers to a set of basic conditions that lenders use to decide whether they can lend you money. These conditions help them assess how likely you are to repay the loan on time. A personal loan is usually unsecured, which means there is no collateral involved, so lenders rely heavily on your financial profile. Meeting these criteria does not always guarantee approval, but it does put you in a strong position.

Income criteria and why it matters

Your income plays a major role in personal loan approval. Lenders want to see a stable and sufficient income that can comfortably support monthly repayments. Whether you are salaried or self-employed, regular cash flow is key.

Most lenders have a minimum monthly income requirement, which may vary based on the city you live in. For example, someone earning Rs. 25,000 per month in a metro city may find it easier to qualify than someone earning the same amount in a smaller town. Your employer’s stability, length of employment, and overall work history also influence how your income is viewed for a personal loan.

Age requirements you should know

Age is another important factor in determining loan eligibility. Typically, lenders prefer applicants who are early in their working years and have enough time before retirement to repay the loan. This usually means a minimum age of around 21 years and a maximum age ranging between 58 and 65 years at loan maturity.

Being within this age bracket reassures lenders that you have a steady earning phase ahead. Younger applicants may get longer tenures, while older applicants may be offered shorter repayment periods for a personal loan.

Credit score and credit history explained simply

Your credit score is like your financial reputation. It reflects how responsibly you have handled credit in the past, including loans and credit cards. A higher score shows timely repayments and sensible borrowing habits.

Most lenders prefer a credit score of 700 or above for better terms. However, even if your score is slightly lower, you may still be considered depending on your income and overall profile. A clean credit history with no major defaults or settlements improves your eligibility for a personal loan significantly.

Existing loans and financial commitments

Lenders also look at your current financial obligations. This includes existing personal loans, home loans, car loans, and credit card dues. They assess how much of your income already goes towards repayments.

A commonly used measure is the fixed obligation to income ratio, which compares your monthly loan commitments to your monthly income. If this ratio is too high, it may affect your ability to get a new personal loan. Keeping your existing EMIs manageable improves your chances.

Employment type and stability

Your employment type gives lenders insight into income stability. Salaried individuals working with established organisations are often seen as lower risk. Self-employed professionals and business owners can also qualify, provided they show consistent income and healthy business finances.

Length of employment or business vintage also matters. Staying with the same employer for at least six to twelve months or running a business for a few years can positively impact your personal loan application.

Documents that support your eligibility

To prove your eligibility, lenders usually ask for basic documents such as identity proof, address proof, income proof, and bank statements. These documents help verify your details and assess your repayment capacity.

Having updated and accurate documents ready can speed up the process and reduce back-and-forth during personal loan processing.

How to apply for instant personal loan smoothly

If you plan to apply for instant personal loan options, preparation is your best friend. Start by checking your credit score and correcting any errors. Make sure your income documents and bank statements reflect a steady inflow of funds.

Avoid applying with multiple lenders at the same time, as too many enquiries can affect your credit profile. Instead, choose platforms that allow you to check offers with minimal impact. Applying for instant personal loan products works best when your profile already matches the lender’s basic requirements.

Improving eligibility for a personal loan over time

If you do not meet the criteria right now, do not worry. Eligibility for a personal loan can be improved with a few smart steps. Paying bills on time, reducing existing debt, and avoiding unnecessary credit can steadily strengthen your profile.

Even small changes, such as lowering credit card usage or increasing savings, can make a noticeable difference over a few months. Think of it as preparing for a future journey where better planning leads to a smoother experience.

Final thoughts on personal loan eligibility

Understanding eligibility for a personal loan helps you make informed decisions and avoid unnecessary rejections. By knowing how income, age, credit score, and financial commitments come together, you can approach a personal loan with clarity and confidence.

A personal loan can be a useful financial tool when used responsibly. With the right preparation and realistic expectations, you can improve your chances and choose an option that fits comfortably into your financial life.

Presented in partnership with Bajaj Finance

title: IILM University Pioneering Educational Excellence: Celebrating 5 years of NEP 2020
content_html:
Launched at a time when India is witnessing rapid digital transformation and preparing to leverage its demographic dividend, National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 has set the stage for a digitally empowered, future-ready generation. As India celebrates five years of the NEP 2020, it is evident that it has redefined the landscape of education. It focuses on quality, inclusivity, and innovation, preparing students to navigate the fast-changing global dynamics with confidence and purpose.

As the nation with the largest population of young learners, India stands at a crucial crossroads, making educational reforms vital for shaping their future and driving national growth and sustainable development. NEP 2020 offers a flexible, multidisciplinary, and skills-driven framework proving that it is a game-changer, emphasizing academic excellence by prioritizing digital literacy, critical thinking, and real-world application of knowledge.

It bridges the gap between the rich Indian Knowledge System and contemporary pedagogy by integrating subjects like holistic well-being, literature, and history with modern, AI and tech-enabled learning. With its focus on upskilling, reskilling, and multilingualism it equips students to effectively meet the complex demands and challenges of this dynamic era.

IILM University: Leading the NEP Vision

At the forefront of this transformative shift stands IILM University Gurugram, a name synonymous with interdisciplinary education, global exposure, and industry readiness. Situated in the heart of the millennium city on Golf Course Road, IILM University offers cutting-edge programs in Management, Law, Computer Science and Engineering, Design, Liberal Arts, Communication and Psychology. With its focus on promoting holistic learning by adopting NEPs ideology of moving away from rote learning and promoting a four-dimensional pedagogical approach that encompasses direct-learning, self-learning, peer learning and experiential learning, IILM University nurtures ethical and future ready leaders.

At IILM University, we are committed to leveraging new age technology to enrich the learning journey. In addition to traditional learning resources, books and periodicals, our library provides seamless access to advanced digital platforms and e-learning portals, ensuring that faculty and students can tap into a wealth of online data and resources. This commitment fosters a culture of continuous learning, innovation, and intellectual curiosity, complementing the vision of NEP 2020.

IILM University has forged a range of strategic collaborations with leading national and international institutions to enrich its academic ecosystem. These partnerships are instrumental in advancing the University’s commitment to sustainability, global exposure, and cutting-edge skill development. By offering micro-credentials in high-demand domains such as cloud computing, data analytics, and digital transformation, IILM University equips students with future-ready competencies aligned with industry needs.

The University also promotes interdisciplinary research and innovation through establishment of Centres of Excellence including Environment and Sustainability, Emotional Intelligence, and Artificial Intelligence. These centres foster critical thinking, experimentation, and socially impactful scholarship.

Digital Transformation

One of the most impactful reforms of NEP 2020 is the digitalization of education. The introduction of the Academic Bank of Credits (ABC) has empowered students with flexibility allowing multiple entry and exit points, the option to pause and resume learning, and the ability to pursue online and offline programs simultaneously. In line with this progressive vision, IILM University has become 100% ABC compliant, adopting the modular structure to offer unparalleled academic flexibility. By integrating multiple entry-exit options across its programs, IILM University is redefining access to quality education. These modular pathways are making education more accessible, inclusive, and tailored to individual needs.

Further advancing this digital momentum, NEP 2020 has promoted the integration of Massive Open Online Courses (MOOCs), virtual classrooms, and AI-driven learning tools breaking down geographical barriers and making high-quality education more affordable and accessible. With a strong emphasis on regional and local languages, the policy ensures that students from diverse socio-economic backgrounds are not left behind in the digital revolution. Reflecting this commitment, IILM University is leveraging technology-enabled learning platforms to foster an inclusive and future-ready academic environment.

All schools at the university have realigned their curricula to reflect NEP’s vision, offering multidisciplinary programs, value-added courses, and experiential learning opportunities. Significant investments have been made in AI-integrated teaching platforms, digital labs, and upgraded academic resources that support cutting-edge research and innovation. In addition, faculty development is central to IILM University’s mission. Through national teaching training programs (MMTTP) and in-house capacity-building, faculty members stay at the cutting edge of pedagogical innovation. 

As India celebrates five-years of NEP 2020 where digital literacy is a pillar of empowerment and progress. IILM University fosters entrepreneurial spirit with robust industry linkages, international university partnerships, and mentorship from eminent experts from diverse sectors to ensure that students are prepared for success.

Empowering a Generation with Deep Purpose

The success of higher education institutions in India lies in their ability to bridge the digital divide. Initiatives like “One Nation, One Data” and “One Nation, One Subscription” have democratized access to academic resources, enabling free access to high-impact research journals and data repositories. This unified approach encourages collaboration among universities and promotes interdisciplinary innovation.

Five years on, NEP 2020 stands as a transformative blueprint for the future of education in India. It has not only reshaped the higher education landscape but has also set the foundation for a digitally empowered and globally competitive generation. At IILM University, we are proud to be at the forefront of this change, fostering innovation, inclusivity, and driving excellence.

As an innovation and purpose driven university, IILM University nurtures young leaders for an ever-changing world by empowering them through tech-enabled knowledge, creativity, and social responsibility. As India advances toward its vision of Viksit Bharat @ 2047, IILM University remains steadfast in its mission to cultivate leaders who are adaptable, ethical, and socially responsible. The university’s holistic approach, rooted in both technological advancement and cultural legacy, places it at the helm of India’s educational transformation. From empowering digital literacy to championing equity and access, IILM University continues to shape minds for a resilient, responsible, and inclusive future.

Launched at a time when India is witnessing rapid digital transformation and preparing to leverage its demographic dividend, National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 has set the stage for a digitally empowered, future-ready generation. As India celebrates five years of the NEP 2020, it is evident that it has redefined the landscape of education. It focuses on quality, inclusivity, and innovation, preparing students to navigate the fast-changing global dynamics with confidence and purpose.
As the nation with the largest population of young learners, India stands at a crucial crossroads, making educational reforms vital for shaping their future and driving national growth and sustainable development. NEP 2020 offers a flexible, multidisciplinary, and skills-driven framework proving that it is a game-changer, emphasizing academic excellence by prioritizing digital literacy, critical thinking, and real-world application of knowledge.
It bridges the gap between the rich Indian Knowledge System and contemporary pedagogy by integrating subjects like holistic well-being, literature, and history with modern, AI and tech-enabled learning. With its focus on upskilling, reskilling, and multilingualism it equips students to effectively meet the complex demands and challenges of this dynamic era.
IILM University: Leading the NEP Vision
At the forefront of this transformative shift stands IILM University Gurugram, a name synonymous with interdisciplinary education, global exposure, and industry readiness. Situated in the heart of the millennium city on Golf Course Road, IILM University offers cutting-edge programs in Management, Law, Computer Science and Engineering, Design, Liberal Arts, Communication and Psychology. With its focus on promoting holistic learning by adopting NEPs ideology of moving away from rote learning and promoting a four-dimensional pedagogical approach that encompasses direct-learning, self-learning, peer learning and experiential learning, IILM University nurtures ethical and future ready leaders.
At IILM University, we are committed to leveraging new age technology to enrich the learning journey. In addition to traditional learning resources, books and periodicals, our library provides seamless access to advanced digital platforms and e-learning portals, ensuring that faculty and students can tap into a wealth of online data and resources. This commitment fosters a culture of continuous learning, innovation, and intellectual curiosity, complementing the vision of NEP 2020.
IILM University has forged a range of strategic collaborations with leading national and international institutions to enrich its academic ecosystem. These partnerships are instrumental in advancing the University’s commitment to sustainability, global exposure, and cutting-edge skill development. By offering micro-credentials in high-demand domains such as cloud computing, data analytics, and digital transformation, IILM University equips students with future-ready competencies aligned with industry needs.
